metadata.subject: Physical and Health Education
metadata.description: Active Living 11 empowers students to design and sustain personally meaningful patterns of physical activity that fit their lives beyond secondary school. Students explore diverse movement experiences, assess personal fitness goals and values, develop lifetime activity plans, and engage with outdoor and traditional movement practices. The course emphasizes student agency in choosing activities, building physical literacy across varied contexts, and understanding how physical activity supports long-term health and well-being. First Peoples perspectives on movement, land connection, and community activity inform the course.
metadata.general_description: A student-directed course developing lifelong physical activity participation, personal fitness planning, and engagement with diverse movement experiences for sustained health and wellness.

big_ideas.statement: Choosing and sustaining personally meaningful physical activities is central to lifelong health and well-being.
big_ideas.elaboration: At Grade 11, students take ownership of their physical activity choices, exploring activities that align with their interests, values, and life circumstances — building habits and identities as physically active people.

big_ideas.statement: Physical literacy — the capacity to move confidently across varied contexts — develops through diverse activity experiences.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students build movement competence and confidence through participation in multiple activity types: individual pursuits, team sports, outdoor recreation, and cultural movement practices.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding personal fitness baselines and goals guides effective activity planning and motivation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students assess their current fitness levels, identify personal health and activity goals, and design realistic plans that accommodate barriers and build progressive commitment.

big_ideas.statement: Outdoor and land-based activities connect physical movement to environmental stewardship and community.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students engage with outdoor recreation, explore First Peoples land connections and traditional activities, and develop environmental awareness through active outdoor participation.

big_ideas.statement: Supporting peers in active living builds community health and strengthens personal commitment to physical activity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop the ability to encourage, guide, and celebrate peers' physical activity participation, creating supportive social environments for lifelong movement.

categories.name: Physical Literacy Development
categories.raw: Developing movement competence, confidence, and participation across varied physical activities.

competencies.raw: Apply fundamental and sport-specific movement skills in diverse physical activities; participate regularly in varied activities; analyze personal movement performance; demonstrate confidence and safety in physical activity contexts.
i_can_statements: I can apply and refine movement skills across multiple activity types — individual sports, team activities, outdoor pursuits, and cultural practices.
I can analyze my own movement performance and identify specific areas for improvement and practice.
I can participate regularly in at least three different types of physical activities that align with my interests and lifestyle.
I can demonstrate confidence and safe practices across familiar and new physical activity contexts.
I can support peers in developing their movement skills through encouragement and constructive feedback.

categories.name: Personal Fitness Planning
categories.raw: Assessing personal fitness, setting achievable goals, and designing sustainable activity plans.

competencies.raw: Assess personal fitness levels and health status; identify personal activity goals and preferences; develop a realistic personal activity plan; monitor progress toward goals; adjust plans based on life circumstances.
i_can_statements: I can assess my current fitness levels across multiple dimensions — cardiovascular fitness, muscular strength, flexibility, and movement quality.
I can identify realistic, personally meaningful activity goals based on my values, interests, and life circumstances.
I can design a personal activity plan that incorporates variety, progressive challenge, and strategies for maintaining motivation.
I can track my participation and progress using methods that work for my life — logs, apps, or personal reflection.
I can adapt my activity plans when barriers emerge — such as seasonal changes, schedule pressures, or injuries.

categories.name: Outdoor and Land-Based Activities
categories.raw: Engaging with outdoor recreation, environmental stewardship, and First Peoples land connections.

competencies.raw: Participate in outdoor recreation activities; develop outdoor skills and safety knowledge; understand environmental stewardship; explore First Peoples land-based practices and perspectives on physical activity.
i_can_statements: I can safely participate in outdoor recreation activities — such as hiking, camping, kayaking, or cycling — using appropriate equipment and techniques.
I can demonstrate knowledge of outdoor safety practices, including risk assessment, emergency preparedness, and navigation skills.
I can explain how outdoor activities connect me to the natural environment and support environmental stewardship.
I can explore and appreciate First Peoples perspectives on land connection, traditional movement practices, and outdoor relationships.
I can advocate for equitable access to outdoor spaces and inclusive outdoor recreation for all community members.

categories.name: Active Living Leadership
categories.raw: Supporting and encouraging others' physical activity participation and building community wellness.

competencies.raw: Support peers in physical activity participation; promote inclusive activity environments; engage with community physical activity opportunities; understand barriers to physical activity participation.
i_can_statements: I can encourage and support peers in finding and sustaining physical activities that align with their interests and abilities.
I can help create inclusive physical activity environments where peers of all abilities feel welcomed and valued.
I can identify and discuss barriers that prevent people from being physically active — including social, economic, and accessibility factors.
I can locate and participate in community physical activity opportunities — such as recreation programs, sports leagues, or outdoor clubs.
I can model positive attitudes toward physical activity and demonstrate commitment to my own lifelong movement participation.

areas.name: Diverse Physical Activities
areas.raw: Participation in varied activity types to build broad physical literacy and discover personal preferences.
topics: Individual pursuits: running, walking, swimming, cycling, martial arts, dance, and yoga
Team and recreational sports: basketball, volleyball, badminton, soccer, ultimate frisbee, and recreational leagues
Outdoor recreation: hiking, camping, kayaking, rock climbing, snowshoeing, and backcountry skiing
Cultural and traditional movement: First Peoples games, social dancing, tai chi, and community-based activities
Fitness activities: strength training, circuit training, aerobic dance, and functional movement
Lifetime activities: golf, pickleball, tennis, table tennis, and activities for lifelong participation

areas.name: Personal Fitness Assessment and Goal Setting
areas.raw: Understanding personal fitness baseline and establishing meaningful activity goals.
topics: Fitness components: cardiovascular fitness, muscular strength, muscular endurance, flexibility, and body composition
Self-assessment methods: conducting and interpreting fitness tests — including step tests, timed runs, strength tests, and flexibility measures
Health and activity questionnaires: understanding personal health history, activity preferences, and readiness for activity
Goal setting frameworks: establishing SMART goals, short-term targets, and long-term lifestyle visions
Motivation and intrinsic goals: exploring personal values and why certain activities matter
Barriers and enablers: identifying personal, social, and environmental factors affecting participation

areas.name: Outdoor Skills and Environmental Stewardship
areas.raw: Developing competence in outdoor recreation and understanding land stewardship responsibilities.
topics: Outdoor safety fundamentals: equipment use, hazard assessment, weather awareness, and emergency response
Navigation and route planning: map reading, compass use, GPS technology, and trail planning
Leave No Trace principles: minimizing environmental impact, ethical outdoor recreation, and land stewardship
First Peoples land-based practices: understanding Indigenous relationships with land, traditional ecological knowledge, and sustainable practices
Outdoor recreation opportunities in BC: exploring local and regional outdoor spaces, accessibility, and equitable access
Environmental awareness: connecting outdoor activities to conservation, climate, and ecosystem health

areas.name: Building and Maintaining Active Habits
areas.raw: Strategies for sustaining physical activity participation and navigating lifestyle changes.
topics: Activity monitoring and tracking: using logs, apps, or wearable technology to track participation
Habit formation: understanding behavioral patterns, establishing routines, and building sustainable activity practices
Motivation management: sustaining intrinsic motivation, renewing commitment, and celebrating progress
Social support: leveraging family, friends, and community connections to support activity
Adapting to transitions: adjusting activity plans during seasonal changes, schedule pressures, or life events
Addressing barriers: problem-solving solutions to obstacles like time, access, cost, or confidence

areas.name: Community and Peer Support in Active Living
areas.raw: Creating supportive environments and fostering peer encouragement for physical activity.
topics: Peer coaching and encouragement: supporting peers' activity participation and building confidence
Inclusive activity environments: understanding and addressing barriers for people of different abilities, backgrounds, and identities
Community resources: exploring local recreation facilities, programs, clubs, and community organizations
Advocacy for active living: promoting school policies and community conditions that support physical activity access
Family engagement: including family members in activity and building household physical activity culture
Digital communities: using social media and online platforms to connect with activity communities and find motivation
